title: Slovakian Chinese Cabbage Dumplings
description: A traditional Slovakian dish featuring Chinese cabbage, these dumplings are resinous and crumbly, perfect for a midnight craving. They are also suitable for a Buddhist temple meal as they are vegetarian and simple.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the chopped Chinese cabbage, flour, salt, and black pepper.
Gradually add the warm water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a tablespoon of chopped onion and garlic in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half moon shape and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan over medium heat and cook the dumplings for 5 minutes on each side.
Serve the dumplings hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
To freeze the dumplings, place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and freeze until solid, then transfer to a freezer safe bag or container.
To cook frozen dumplings, simply thaw and cook as directed above.
